property.name: dfs.nameservices
property.description: The logical nameservice ID for the HDFS HA cluster.

property.name: dfs.ha.namenodes.mycluster
property.description: The two NameNodes belonging to the logical nameservice.

property.name: dfs.namenode.http-address.mycluster.nn1
property.description: Web HTTP console address for NameNode 1.

property.name: dfs.namenode.http-address.mycluster.nn2
property.description: Web HTTP console address for NameNode 2.

property.name: dfs.namenode.shared.edits.dir
property.description: Quorum Journal Manager (QJM) shared edits URI. Pointing to the 3 JournalNode daemons.

property.name: dfs.client.failover.proxy.provider.mycluster
property.description: The Java class HDFS clients use to determine which NameNode is active and route requests.

property.name: dfs.ha.fencing.methods
property.description: Fencing methods used to isolate a dead Active NameNode. In local Docker labs, a dummy shell fence is used. In production, sshfence or ipofence would power down nodes.

property.name: dfs.ha.automatic-failover.enabled
property.description: Enables automatic failover via the ZooKeeper Failover Controller (ZKFC).

property.name: dfs.namenode.name.dir
property.description: Local filesystem path where the NameNode metadata is written.

property.name: dfs.datanode.data.dir
property.description: Local filesystem path where DataNodes store HDFS blocks.

property.name: dfs.replication
property.description: Default HDFS block replication factor (configured as 2 for local Docker topology verification).
